description: My overall experience with NAKIVO Backup & Replication has been outstanding. It's by far the best backup solution I've ever used—reliable, efficient, and packed with powerful features. I now confidently recommend it to my friends and colleagues.
author: Abdulla M.
pros: It offers excellent user-friendliness, a clean and intuitive user interface, is easy to manage and maintain, packed with useful features, and remains cost-effective, making it a great overall choice.
cons: At the moment, I don’t have any additional points as everything I can think of is already included as a feature.

description: From customer support, technical support to licensing makes Nakivo the best solution on the market. It also allows easy integration with other third party solutions such as Active Directory.
author: Wisdom Q.
pros: Nakivo Backup and Replication is easy to implement, has a friendly user interface and it is easy to use.
cons: I would like the Nakivo team to explore backing up or replicating supporting platforms such as the IBM AIX servers

review_title: Reliable, Cost-Effective Backup That Just Works
description: Overall, it’s been a dependable backup solution that does exactly what we need it to do. It provides strong VM protection, reliable restores, and good performance without the overhead or pricing of some larger enterprise platforms. For our environment, it strikes the right balance between capability and simplicity.
author: Mark A.
pros: Reliable, straightforward, and "cost-effective." Deployment was simple, the interface is clean, and backup jobs are easy to manage. It gives us solid protection without unnecessary complexity. Customer support has been great!
cons: I would like to see a better scheduling interface or options. Also, monitoring for Hyper V environment which is the only reason I don't give 5 stars ;-)

author: Giordano P.
pros: I use Nakivo to backup AWS istances from a region to another and works very well. It is easy to use and configure.
cons: Enable the possibility to restore guest file from a replicated istances to restore file deleted or damage

author: Tal Y.
pros: I’ve been testing out NAKIVO lately, and honestly, it’s probably the only backup solution I’ve found that gives you full web-based management from start to finish. You can do everything - setup, scheduling, recovery, and monitoring - straight from the browser. I couldn’t find anything close to that in the open-source world. It’s also super easy to maintain and troubleshoot. The logs are clear and detailed, and the docs actually make sense (which is rare). I had a working backup for my Linux server in under five minutes, even without knowing the product beforehand - that’s how straightforward it is.
cons: The only downside is the price, which can feel a bit steep if you’re just using it for a home lab. But considering how solid and long-term the solution is, I’d say it’s totally worth paying a bit more for peace of mind and ease of use.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: so far so good. The Nakivo company are easy to deal with and the license delivery is straightforward.
author: Caeilte R.
pros: Can be hosted in TrueNas which is useful as a separate VM (jail in BSD) ; so target for backups is separate device with the nakivo hosted on it
cons: Sometimes error messages dont help such as "Internal error" when a VM back up job fails. It would be more useful if all errors encountered with a vm backup job gave meaningful messages

review_title: Efficient, Reliable, and User-Friendly Backup Solution with Strong Recovery Features
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I liked NAKIVO Backup & Replication most for its simplicity and efficiency, with a clean, intuitive web interface, flexible deployment options (NAS, Linux, Windows, or virtual appliance), application-aware backups for SQL, Exchange, and Active Directory, instant VM recovery, granular restores, and robust features like immutable repositories, encryption, and global deduplication that ensure fast, reliable, and secure data protection.
cons: What I liked least about NAKIVO Backup & Replication is that some advanced features require higher license editions, reporting and monitoring are limited, and complex deployments may need more guidance.

description: Easy to install, easy to use, with reliable and fast-reacting support, always friendly and also providing remote sessions.
author: David G.
pros: Works for years without any problem. Support is always reachable and reacts fast. Transporters for many systems are available, great for our structure, where the backup storage is directly connected to a single server and not in the same network as our virtualisation machines.
cons: Make it easier to restore single files from Linux machines, it does not support direct downloads on the web GUI when source is ext4.

description: It's worked well for us, we switched from CloudberryLab (MSP360) and us BackBlaze B2. It took minimal time to setup and was working within 30 mins of starting setup.
author: John K.
pros: This product was very easy to setup, the support I got was also quick and no AI bots to talk to. I always prefer working with companies that use live agents.
cons: I wish it was a none web page for the panel. Not a big deal though. I would also suggest more explanation on certain features. Again, not a deal breaker.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Reliable, Easy-to-Use, and Cost-Effective Backup Solution
description: Overall, my experience with NAKIVO Backup & Replication has been very positive. It has helped simplify backup management, improve recovery readiness, and provide a dependable solution for protecting critical systems. The product feels mature, practical, and well suited for organizations that want strong backup and disaster recovery capabilities without excessive complexity or cost.
author: Matteo P.
pros: What I liked most about NAKIVO Backup & Replication is its simplicity and reliability. The interface is clean, easy to navigate, and makes it straightforward to configure backup, replication, and recovery tasks. I also appreciate the flexible deployment options and the ability to manage backups efficiently without adding unnecessary complexity.
cons: One area that could be improved is the reporting and alerting, which could be a bit more detailed out of the box. In larger environments, the web interface can sometimes feel slightly less responsive than expected, but overall this has not had a major impact on daily operations.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I've been having an good overall experience with Nakivo, reliable backups, that I can recover easily and the support are really fast to respond and to sort my issue out.
author: César S.
pros: I like how reliable and fast the backups are made and how easy it is to recovery them
cons: Not being able to use the calendar by default

review_title: Produce only if NAKIVO is with you :)
description: NAKIVO helped me in the event of a host failure as well as damage to the virtual machines. In these cases, a previously prepared backup helped.
author: Mawos-Radosław P.
pros: The most important thing is the reliability of this tool. It has saved me several times in my work.
cons: I don't see any disadvantages of NAKIVO. For me it would be easier to have backups in the form of easily transferable files than a complicated repository, but of course I appreciate functionality and reliability above all.

description: Our overall experience is pretty good especially when it comes to support as the support team is very helpfull and fast when you have an issue or question.
author: Emmanouel P.
pros: We use Nakivo to back up our VPS servers and NAS devices. The UI and features are quite satisfactory with several expansion possibilities. We liked the new MSP feature where you can give specific access to users for specific machines.
cons: There are features that we would like to be integrated such as the ability to exclude directories, the creation of an image for bare metal restore as well as the creation of Plesk integration to use Nakivo as a Plesk backup tool.

review_title: The best Support and configuration tools
description: Very satsfying, they offer the best support in the segment
author: Gabriel G.
pros: From all the Backup sollutions that i tryed or searched, Nakivo was the best all around software in the market. Good prices, outstanding support, easy and secure configurations, and a very intuitive plataform. Easy choice for me
cons: Until now, no dislikes to report. Just waiting until the release of the Proxmox Backup.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Our experience with NAKIVO Backup & Replication has been fantastic. It has provided our MSP with a reliable, cost-effective, and scalable backup solution. The support team is responsive, and updates frequently bring valuable enhancements. Unlike other backup solutions, NAKIVO stands out for its simplicity, efficiency, and affordability. Highly recommended for MSPs looking for an easy-to-manage backup and disaster recovery solution!
author: Chris G.
pros: NAKIVO Backup & Replication is a game-changer for our MSP. It offers an intuitive interface, fast backup speeds, and a robust feature set, all at an excellent price point. The ease of deployment and management, along with reliable VM backups and recovery, make it a standout solution. The ransomware protection and built-in replication features give us added confidence in our disaster recovery strategy.
cons: While NAKIVO is excellent, the reporting and alerting functionalities could be more detailed. Also, native cloud-to-cloud backup options for more platforms would be a welcome addition in future updates."

review_title: Nakivo should be take for encryption and Backup
description: all over experience is very good person are very helpful in understanding about product and support
author: Deepak S.
pros: Backup & Data security, Encryption. console is very good for understanding about each and everything about Nakivo in one page. Microsoft office data also we can take backup with security.
cons: take time in renewal because we have to do it fast because of this process client can lost data of renewal period. so need to take care about this.

author: Andrea M.
pros: I have been using Nakivo for about a year now (I used Veeam) and have found the backup performance remarkable, the interface is very simple and user-friendly and it is easy to create your backup plan. Very happy with the choice I made. Recommended!
cons: In the preivous month I didn't find major faults

review_title: Secure and fast backups to manage.
description: NAKIVO Backup & Replication is my main backup solution, I use the recommended 3,2,1 scheme by creating three types of backups in different locations and applying immutability on one of the repositories.
author: Faustino D.
pros: The ease of creating immutable backups, with the ability to manage different backup schemes from a single console.
cons: Real-time replication features for Nutanix hypervisors.

description: The backup job feature is my favorite. Creating, editing and restoring jobs is no problem at all.
author: Udo B.
pros: Nakivo is a very user-friendly backup solution. Installation is no problem for a reasonably trained system administrator. The use is more or less self-explanatory, but of course there is also a very good manual and an equally good knowledge base that leaves nothing to be desired. The support, which I have only had to use once in five years, is also very competent.
cons: There is really nothing which i do not like.

author: Keiren G.
pros: The quick overview dashboard is great for snap shot overview of the backup system, I really like that the issue are listed on the homepage and have good kb to help fix them
cons: Please please we need Google workspace mailbox and Google drive backups to be supported by Nakivo.

review_title: Perfect for VM Backup and Replication
description: The prouct works well at a fraction of the price compared with other solutions such as Zerto
author: James B.
pros: Great product that helps us easily meet our backup and replication requirements. The product is easy to setup/configure and manage via a nice web UI. The API helps us manage and monitor the backup and replications.
cons: The product could provide better visibility into the storage usage of VMs being backed up. When using forever incremental backups, there is no reporting capability making it difficult to account for storage on a per VM/customer level.

author: Rabih S.
pros: full and incremental options. This ensures all your important data is safe without taking up too much storage space. The backups and recoveries are very fast, so you don’t have to worry about long downtimes. The software is easy to use, even if you don’t have a lot of technical knowledge. The interface is clear and straightforward. You can create virtual copies of your physical machines, which is great for disaster recovery and testing
cons: The advanced features require some technical know-how, which means the software is powerful and capable of handling complex backup needs. This depth can be a positive aspect for businesses looking to fully leverage their backup solutions.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: It is easy both backup and restore. The backup machine can be choose up to 5
author: Krachang K.
pros: It is easy to backup and restore. It is cheaper than the other.
cons: Restore SQL Database has to find database by manual

author: Maciej K.
pros: ease of use and intuitive configuration, ease of data recovery
cons: new subscription model, but now everyone is using it. As far as functionality is concerned, everything is fine.

review_title: Affordable, yet robust and fast working backup appliance
author: Michał M.
pros: It is running under linux, therefore you can deploy it really fast. The Director and Transporter paradigm makes it convinient to set up in different environments, from air gapped clusters, to edge servers and more.
cons: It does not support RBD as a storage repository out of the box, I am still waiting on that option to be available.
